body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Change the physics of the OceananigansSimulation model, per Simone's suggestions. This is more similar to the model setup in the ClimaOcean [one degree example](https://clima.github.io/ClimaOceanDocumentation/stable/literated/one_degree_simulation/), but not exactly the same.

Taken from #1501 to make that PR smaller.
This may be behavior changing for CMIP, but I don't see any changes visually in the buildkite plots.
title: change Oceananigans model closures

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Provide downward shortwave and longwave radiative fluxes to surface models, instead of using F_radiative.

After some upstream changes the downgrade test is failing due to StaticArrays, so I bumped the compat.

## To-do
- [x] don't get F_radiative from the atmosphere
- [x] pass SW_d, LW_d to component models
- [x] use these to compute net radiation correctly

## To do in future PRs
- [ ] store SW_d, LW_d in bucket cache instead of in BucketSimulation (requires ClimaLand change)


<!---

I have:

- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: fix radiative flux over surface models
